title: Parenting Materials - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Parenting & Childcare > Parenting Materials 26 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Disabilities Issues Hispanic/Latino Community Native American Community DOUGLAS EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT TAKE ROOTS PARENTING CONNECTIONS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offering parent resources and support for education and family engagement activities. 1409 NE Diamond Lake Boulevard Suite 110 Roseburg, OR 97470 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm Email (541) 957-4819 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILIES FIRST PARENT RESOURCE CENTER PARENTING CLASSES AND RESOURCE LIBRARY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides two active parenting series each year. Also offers multiple parent-child playgroup and workshop opportunities, including divorce education and prenatal classes. 401 S Canyon Boulevard John Day, OR 97845 Eligibility: Varies by service Hours: No standard hours Email (541) 575-1006 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FOSTERCLUB FOSTER PARENT ONLINE TRAINING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF The online training is a subscription-based service offering interactive educational modules for credit to resource parents, supportive adults, and child welfare professionals. To confirm if earned credits are accepted, contact your local agency. 620 S Holladay Drive #1 Seaside, OR 97138 Eligibility: Resource parents, supportive adults and/or child welfare professionals Hours: Not applicable Email (503) 717-1552 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FOUR RIVERS EARLY LEARNING AND PARENTING PARENTING RESOURCES AND SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Parenting support group offering resources, referrals, and evidence based parenting classes. Helps build strong relationships with children. 400 E Scenic Drive Suite 207 The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Parents or guardians Hours: Varies Email (541) 506-2234 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GLADSTONE CENTER FOR CHILDREN AND FAMILIES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides parenting materials, family events, and a free lending library. It also hosts a variety of classes and groups offered by the agencies within the center. The agencies it houses include the Gladstone Health & Wellness Center, pediatric health, emotional and dental services. Family Stepping Stones, Relief Nursery, Head Start and Early Head Start, Northwest Family services navigators, Clackamas Education Service District EI/ECSE program, LifeWorks Northwest family counseling, WIC, SMART, Clackamas Community College GED program, and the Gladstone School District kindergarten program. 18905 Portland Avenue Gladstone, OR 97027 Eligibility: Households with children from birth through kindergarten Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-4pm, also evening hours vary Email (503) 496-3939 Get Directions Visit Website More Details GRANTS PASS SCHOOL DISTRICT 7 PARENTING WORKSHOPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers evidence-based discipline strategies, teaches skills to help empower and encourage healthy parenting, also helps build a sense of community with other parents or guardians. Dinner and child care are provided. 725 North East Dean Drive Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Parents or guardians Hours: No standard hours Email (541) 474-5700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HACIENDA COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION SEMBRANDO SEMILLITAS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides early childhood education to parents, using the Nurturing ParentingTM curriculum, offering Spanish-English educational materials and activities that promote physical, cognitive and emotional development. Parents participate in weekly parenting support groups, educational workshops, and twice-monthly home visits from Hacienda's Parent Educators, monitoring their child's health and development. Children and infants engage in activities geared toward cognitive health and kindergarten preparation. Children receive preventative health and developmental screenings and referral resources. 6700 NE Killingsworth Street Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Pregnant women and parents of children ages 0-5 who qualify as low-income (WIC or SNAP recipients) and identify as Latino Hours: Monday-Thursday 9am-5pm Email (503) 781-1466 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOPE FAMILY CARE CENTER COLUMBIA GORGE PREGNANCY RESOURCE CENTERS THE DALLES PREGNANCY AND PARENT SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A faith-based organization that provides information and referrals to those experiencing unplanned pregnancies. Services include pregnancy testing, pro-life counseling, parenting support, and community referrals. Does not recommend or refer to abortion services. Individuals do not have to participate in classes to be eligible to receive maternity and infant clothes, diapers, and other infant care needs. 3206 W 6th Street Ste 200 The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Expectant families and new parents Hours: Tuesday/Wednesday/Thursday 10am-5pm Email (541) 296-0650 Get Directions Visit Website More Details HOPE FAMILY CARE CENTER PREGNANCY AND PARENT SUPPORT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A faith-based organization that provides information and referrals to those experiencing unplanned pregnancies. Services include pregnancy testing, pro-life counseling, parenting support, and community referrals. Does not recommend or refer to abortion services. Individuals do not have to participate in classes to be eligible to receive maternity and infant clothes, diapers, and other infant care needs. 1942 12th Street Hood River, OR 97031 Eligibility: Expectant families and new parents Hours: Tuesday/Wednesday/Thursday 10am-5pm Email (541) 386-1050 Get Directions Visit Website More Details INSIGHTS YOUNG PARENT SERVICES PARENTING RESOURCES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a resource closet of baby clothing and toys for enrolled program participants. 4900 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Parents age 22 and younger Hours: Phone: Monday-Friday 9am-4pm (503) 239-6996 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MALHEUR EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT EASTERN OREGON COMMUNITY BASED SERVICES HUB Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers support for school readiness, including parenting materials to support early learning. 363 A Street W Vale, OR 97918 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4pm (541) 473-3138 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MARION AND POLK EARLY LEARNING HUB LANTERN Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers parenting tips on topics such as sleep, eating, potty training, tantrums, and text information on local resources and events. 2995 Ryan Drive SE Suite 100 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Ages 0-11 and their caregivers. Child's age and zipcode are required. Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 485-3219 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MORRISON CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES MORRISON CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES ABC LISTOS PARA APRENDER Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides parenting trainings, educational home visits, parenting issues support groups, parenting materials, referrals, case management, and developmental screening. Offers parents support with guidance on how to deal with a child's tantrums, questions about a child's developmental stages, and alternative types of discipline. 7820 NE Holman Street Suite B5 Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: Spanish speaking parents with children ages 0-5 or who are pregnant Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm, some evening appointments available Email (503) 258-4200 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MOTHER & CHILD EDUCATION CENTER PREGNANCY AND PARENTING SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Pregnancy supplies include pre-natal vitamins, morning sickness kits, pregnancy pillows, maternity clothing, and pregnancy and parenting books. Staff are available to meet with clients to answer pregnancy and parenting questions or provide referrals to other programs. 1515 NE 41st Avenue Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Parenting classes offered for children up to the age of 2 Hours: Helpline: 7 days per week In person Monday-Thursday 10am-5pm, Friday 10am-3pm Email (503) 249-5801 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN REHABILITATION ASSOCIATION OF THE NORTHWEST NARA NW CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES GRESHAM CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health assessment and evaluation, counseling (individual, couples, family, and group), case management, cultural groups and activities, Native parenting classes, foster parent groups, access to physical health services, Healthy Native Kids (OHP/CHIPRA) insurance assistance, and wraparound care for Native youth with multiple needs. Referrals to other resources provided. Also provides diapers. Staff receive training on cultural competence to provide cultural programming for clients. Staff includes Master's-level counselors and social workers, certified alcohol and drug counselors (CADC), psychologists and child psychiatrists, and nurse practitioners. Student interns studying to become CADCs or counselors may also work under supervision of licensed staff. The program is certified by the State of Oregon to provide mental health and addiction treatment services for all ages. 620 NE 2nd Street Gresham, OR 97030 Eligibility: Wraparound care: Youth who identify as Native American ages 9-21 All other services: Families with minor children who identify as Native American Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 953-6598 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NORTHWEST PARENTING PARENT TRAINING AND INFORMATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers parenting education programs, workshops, child development classes, support groups, parent–child activity groups, educational materials, and specialized information and referral. Divorce, custody, and court-approved classes are available on the website. 5825 NE Ray Circle Hillsboro, OR 97124 Eligibility: Families with children under age 18 Hours: No standard hours Email (503) 614-3188 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY SUPPORT NETWORK MID-OREGON REGION OFFICE REACH OUT OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a parent warmline offering peer support and connections to local resources, including one-to-one support, resource navigation, support groups, training, and advocacy. 4263 Commercial Street SE Suite 300 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Parents and caregivers raising children and youth with emotional, mental, behavioral, or other health needs Hours: Reach Out Oregon: Monday-Friday 10am-4pm. Closed holidays. Office: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (833) 732-2467 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY SUPPORT NETWORK NORTH OFFICE REACH OUT OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a parent warmline offering peer support and connections to local resources, including one-to-one support, resource navigation, support groups, training, and advocacy. 7110 SW Fir Loop Suite 160 Tigard, OR 97223 Eligibility: Parents and caregivers raising children and youth with emotional, mental, behavioral, or other health needs Hours: Reach Out Oregon: Monday-Friday 10am-4pm. Closed holidays. Office: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (833) 732-2467 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON FAMILY SUPPORT NETWORK SOUTH OREGON REACH OUT OREGON Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a parent warmline offering peer support and connections to local resources, including one-to-one support, resource navigation, support groups, training, and advocacy. 72A Centennial Loop Suite 150 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Parents and caregivers raising children and youth with emotional, mental, behavioral, or other health needs Hours: Reach Out Oregon: Monday-Friday 10am-4pm. Closed holidays. Office: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (833) 732-2467 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON PARENTING EDUCATION COLLABORATIVE HUB RESOURCE DIRECTORY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Parenting hub network offers parenting resources, groups, workshops, and classes. Eligibility: Must be a parent or guardian Hours: Varies by service Email Get Directions Visit Website More Details OSU EXTENSION SERVICE PARENTING Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides downloadable materials about parenting, support groups and detailed information about grandparents or older relatives raising children. 118 Ballard Hall Corvallis, OR 97331 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable (541) 737-2713 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PARENTING NOW PARENTING GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides parenting groups that follow a professionally developed curriculum and include group sessions, parent booklets, e-newsletters, and other materials. 86 Centennial Loop Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Varies by class, most classes for parents with children ages 0-8 years old Hours: Class schedules vary Email (541) 484-5316 302 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ROBERT WOOD JOHNSON FOUNDATION SESAME WORKSHOP Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides resources and activities for school readiness, language and literacy, art, math, science, financial education, social and emotional skills, and health and wellness. Available as printable materials, interactive games, storybooks, videos, courses, and webinars. 50 College Road E Princeton, NJ 085406614 Eligibility: Unrestricted; focus is children ages 1-7 Hours: Not applicable Email (877) 843-7953 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UNITED WAY OF LANE COUNTY EARLY CHILDHOOD HUB OF LANE COUNTY Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides parenting resources and classes, a calendar of community and family events, and a variety of topical and age-based articles on parenting, and more. 3171 Gateway Loop Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Varies depending on classes or events Email (541) 741-6000 Get Directions Visit Website More Details VROOM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides age-appropriate brain-building activities for adults to help children learn and develop. App and website include instructional tools, parenting materials, videos, and educational resources. Website Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: No standard hours Email 48258 Visit Website More Details ZERO TO THREE PARENTING EDUCATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information on parenting, child development, and early learning. 1255 23rd Street NW Suite 350 Washington, DC 20037 Eligibility: Unrestricted Hours: Not applicable (202) 638-1144 Get Directions Visit Website More Details DOUGLAS EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT TAKE ROOTS PARENTING CONNECTIONS FAMILIES FIRST PARENT RESOURCE CENTER PARENTING CLASSES AND RESOURCE LIBRARY FOSTERCLUB FOSTER PARENT ONLINE TRAINING FOUR RIVERS EARLY LEARNING AND PARENTING PARENTING RESOURCES AND SUPPORT GLADSTONE CENTER FOR CHILDREN AND FAMILIES GLADSTONE CENTER FOR CHILDREN AND FAMILIES GRANTS PASS SCHOOL DISTRICT 7 PARENTING WORKSHOPS HACIENDA COMMUNITY DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION SEMBRANDO SEMILLITAS HOPE FAMILY CARE CENTER PREGNANCY AND PARENT SUPPORT SERVICES HOPE FAMILY CARE CENTER PREGNANCY AND PARENT SUPPORT SERVICES INSIGHTS YOUNG PARENT SERVICES PARENTING RESOURCES MALHEUR EDUCATION SERVICE DISTRICT EASTERN OREGON COMMUNITY BASED SERVICES HUB MARION AND POLK EARLY LEARNING HUB LANTERN MORRISON CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES LISTOS PARA APRENDER MOTHER & CHILD EDUCATION CENTER PREGNANCY AND PARENTING SUPPORT NATIVE AMERICAN REHABILITATION ASSOCIATION OF THE NORTHWEST CHILD AND FAMILY SERVICES NORTHWEST PARENTING PARENT TRAINING AND INFORMATION OREGON FAMILY SUPPORT NETWORK REACH OUT OREGON OREGON FAMILY SUPPORT NETWORK REACH OUT OREGON OREGON FAMILY SUPPORT NETWORK REACH OUT OREGON OSU EXTENSION SERVICE PARENTING PARENTING NOW PARENTING GROUPS UNITED WAY OF LANE COUNTY EARLY CHILDHOOD HUB OF LANE COUNTY
